bullets.content: IF user requests account-specific actions (exchange/return/modify/cancel) → THEN ask for first name, last name, and zip code before proceeding
IF user refuses to provide zip code → THEN ask for the user's email address instead
IF provided first name, last name, and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip)
IF provided email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email)
IF find_user_id returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and payment methods
IF acting on a specific order or item → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each relevant order_id before taking action
IF user asks about product options or replacement variants → THEN call list_all_product_types() (if needed) and call get_product_details(product_id) to get variant availability, options, and prices
IF user requests an exchange for an item already in an order → THEN look up the product_id from the order details and call get_product_details(product_id) to identify suitable replacement item_ids
IF order status is "delivered" AND user requests an exchange of delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order status is "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order status is "pending" AND user requests to change item variants → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order status is "pending" AND user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
IF a replacement variant must be chosen → THEN verify variant availability and price via get_product_details(product_id) and confirm selection with the user before calling any exchange/modify tool
IF an exchange/return involves a price difference → THEN include the payment_method_id in the exchange/return call and use the specified payment method to process the payment or refund

IF executing any order-changing tool call → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id/get_user_details) → fetch relevant order_details → check product/variant availability and price → obtain/confirm payment method → then call the appropriate action tool
IF a replacement variant is found → THEN present the variant's size/compatibility/backlight/etc. and price and ask the user "Do you want this one?" before proceeding

IF an exchange results in a price difference → THEN compute the difference, tell the user whether they owe money or will receive a refund (include the amount), and ask which payment method to use for payment/refund
IF initiating a return or exchange → THEN tell the user they will receive an email with return instructions

IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) has been successfully submitted → THEN inform the user it has been processed (e.g., "it's been processed" / "return requested" / "cancelled") and ask if they need anything else

IF the user's requested refund destination is not allowed (e.g., different original payment methods across orders) → THEN explain the limitation and state the allowed options (refund to the original payment method or to a gift card)
IF user requests cancellation → THEN ask for the reason for cancelling before calling cancel_pending_order
IF user identification (first name, last name, zip OR email) is not provided → THEN do not fetch or act on account/orders
IF refund is requested → THEN refunds are only allowed to the original payment method used for that order or to a gift card; refunds cannot be routed to another order's different payment method
IF order status is not "pending" → THEN do not call cancel_pending_order (only pending orders are cancellable)
IF order status is "delivered" → THEN use return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items (do not use modify_pending_order_items for delivered orders)
IF a desired replacement variant's "available" field is false → THEN inform the user that the variant is out of stock and do not select it for exchange/modify

deltas.content: IF user requests an address correction for their account and/or pending orders → THEN for each pending order call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) and call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show address updates for multiple pending orders and the user account (conversation 41); the playbook lacked a specific rule to perform these update calls.

deltas.content: IF executing any order-changing tool call → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id/get_user_details) → fetch relevant order_details → check product/variant availability and price → obtain/confirm payment method → verify the selected payment_method_id is valid (exists in get_user_details.payment_methods or matches the order's original payment method in payment_history); if invalid or missing, ask the user to confirm which payment method to use → then call the appropriate action tool.
deltas.rationale: A tool call failed because an invalid payment_method_id was used; the sequence must explicitly require validating the payment method before calling action tools (conversation 46).

deltas.content: IF user asks how many product options or variants are available → THEN call list_all_product_types() (if needed) and get_product_details(product_id) and report the count of variants whose 'available' field is true (i.e., available for selection).
deltas.rationale: When asked for available options the assistant reported a count reflecting only available variants; the playbook should specify counting available=true variants when answering availability queries (conversation 3).

deltas.content: IF initiating a return or exchange → THEN tell the user they will receive an email with return/exchange instructions; also confirm the refund/payment destination (original payment method or gift card) and, if the user asked about timing, provide the expected refund/payment timing.
deltas.rationale: Some trajectories omitted informing the user about return instructions by email or failed to confirm refund destination/timing; confirmation must explicitly include email notice and refund destination/timing when requested (conversations 19 and 46).

deltas.content: IF user requests an address change → THEN ask whether to apply the change to all pending orders and whether to update the user account address as well before making any modify calls.
deltas.rationale: In a trajectory the assistant asked whether to update all pending orders and the user address and proceeded on confirmation; playbook should require this confirmation to avoid unintended changes (conversation 41).

deltas.content: IF user requests to update their address for their account and/or orders → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id_by_name_zip/find_user_id_by_email → get_user_details) → fetch relevant order_details for each relevant order_id → for each order with status 'pending' call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address → if the user requests updating their account address, call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) → for orders not in 'pending' status, inform the user those orders' shipping addresses cannot be modified and offer alternatives (e.g., contact carrier, initiate return/exchange after delivery).
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents updating both pending order shipping addresses and the user's account address; the playbook lacked a clear rule and required sequence for address updates and handling of non-pending orders.

deltas.content: IF order status is not 'pending' → THEN do not call modify_pending_order_address (only pending orders' shipping addresses can be modified); instead inform the user that processed/delivered orders cannot have their shipping address changed and provide alternatives.
deltas.rationale: Agents only modified pending order addresses in the conversations; there is no existing precondition that explicitly forbids calling modify_pending_order_address for non-pending orders.

deltas.content: IF a replacement variant is found → THEN present the variant's size/compatibility/backlight/etc. and price and ask the user 'Do you want this one?' before proceeding; alternatively, if the user explicitly provides a payment/refund method or explicitly says they want you to proceed (e.g., 'yes, please proceed' or provides the payment method), treat that as confirmation and proceed.
deltas.rationale: In trajectories, users sometimes confirmed variant selection implicitly by providing the payment/refund method or saying to proceed, so the rule should allow explicit payment method or a clear proceed instruction to serve as confirmation.

deltas.content: IF user requests cancellation → THEN ask for the reason for cancelling before calling cancel_pending_order; if the user does not provide a reason but otherwise confirms they want the cancellation to proceed (e.g., by responding 'yes, go ahead'), the agent may proceed using a default reason such as 'no longer needed' and document that reason in the cancel_pending_order call.
deltas.rationale: Agents processed cancellations when the user confirmed proceeding but did not state a cancellation reason; requiring a reason but allowing a documented default when none is provided matches observed behavior and avoids blocking cancellations.

deltas.content: IF a replacement variant is found → THEN present the variant's size/compatibility/backlight/etc. and price and ask the user 'Do you want this one?' before proceeding, UNLESS the user has explicitly requested that specific variant/item_id or has already explicitly confirmed that exact replacement — in that case, present the variant details and proceed without an additional yes/no prompt.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users sometimes provide an explicit item_id to swap to; requiring an extra 'Do you want this one?' in those cases is redundant and the agent proceeded correctly when the user already requested a specific variant.

deltas.content: IF the user cannot complete payment through stored payment methods and requests to provide card details outside the chat (e.g., over the phone) OR does not want to save card information online → THEN offer to transfer the user to a human agent to complete the payment securely, and explain that this option is available if they prefer not to add payment details in-chat.
deltas.rationale: A user requested to give card details over the phone when no saved payment method was available; the agent offered transfer to a human, which is an appropriate fallback not currently in the playbook.

deltas.content: IF the user refuses to provide a new shipping address aloud but indicates a desired address is already saved in their account → THEN look up the user's saved addresses and offer the available saved addresses (or the specific saved address the user referenced) for selection/confirmation; if the user explicitly authorizes using one of the saved addresses, proceed to modify the order address using that saved address.
deltas.rationale: A user declined to speak the new address and asked the agent to use a saved address; agents looked up and used the saved order address. The playbook should allow using saved addresses with user confirmation or explicit authorization.

deltas.content: IF initiating a return or refund → THEN compute and tell the user the exact refund amount AND provide an estimated processing time for the refund to reach the chosen/original payment method (e.g., 'it can be done within X days'), and confirm the refund destination.
deltas.rationale: Users asked for and were given the refund amount and an estimated timeframe in the trajectories; the playbook should require communicating both amount and expected processing time when initiating refunds/returns.

deltas.content: IF a user requests to use a gift card to pay for an exchange/return or to cover a price difference → THEN check the user's saved payment methods for a gift_card entry and verify the gift card's balance is sufficient to cover the required amount. If the balance is insufficient, inform the user of the shortfall and ask which alternate payment method they want to use (or whether to split payment). Confirm the chosen payment method before proceeding.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users explicitly asking to pay with a gift card and the agent verifying the gift card balance before processing (conversation 58). The playbook lacked an explicit rule to verify gift card sufficiency and handle shortfalls.

deltas.content: IF executing any order-changing tool call → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id/get_user_details) → fetch relevant order_details → check product/variant availability and price → obtain/confirm payment method (if the payment method is a gift_card, verify the gift card balance is sufficient and confirm with the user; if insufficient, request an alternate payment method or split payment) → then call the appropriate action tool.
deltas.rationale: Update the general execution sequence to explicitly require gift card balance verification when the selected payment method is a gift card, as seen in conversation 58 where the agent confirmed gift card balance before charging it.

deltas.content: IF order status is 'pending' AND user requests to change the shipping address for that order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) after identifying the user and fetching order_details. Confirm the updated shipping address with the user and ensure the order remains pending before making the change.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories include changing the shipping address on a pending order via modify_pending_order_address (conversation 110), but the playbook did not include guidance for address modifications on pending orders.

deltas.content: IF a user requests to update their account's default shipping address → THEN identify the user (find_user_id/get_user_details), confirm the new address details with the user, call update_user_default_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) (or the equivalent tool), and confirm to the user that the default address has been updated for future orders.
deltas.rationale: Users requested that their default address be updated for future orders (conversation 110). The playbook did not include a rule for updating a user's default account address.

deltas.content: IF a user has multiple orders and requests an account-specific action on an order or item → THEN ask the user to specify which order (order ID) they want to act on or explicitly confirm which of their orders is the target before fetching order details or making any changes.
deltas.rationale: Agents must confirm the correct order when users have multiple orders to avoid acting on the wrong order (observed: agent modified a different order than the one the user discussed).

deltas.content: IF a user requests an exchange to a different product category/type (e.g., luggage -> coat) → THEN inform the user that exchanges are limited to variants of the same product/product type (i.e., same product_id variants) and offer to process a return or cancel the order and place a new order for the different product type instead.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show exchanges being refused when the requested replacement is a different product type; playbook should explicitly state this limitation and the alternatives.

deltas.content: IF order status is 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt to reinstate the order via automated tools; inform the user that cancelled orders cannot be reopened automatically and offer to place a new order or escalate to a human agent for special handling.
deltas.rationale: Users attempted to undo a cancellation; the system cannot reinstate cancelled orders automatically and should escalate or offer alternatives.

deltas.content: IF the user's requested refund destination is not allowed (e.g., different original payment methods across orders) → THEN explain the limitation, state the allowed options (refund to the original payment method or to a gift card), and ask the user to confirm which of the allowed options they want before proceeding. If the user requests a disallowed destination, explicitly confirm that you will use the allowed option chosen by the user prior to executing the refund.
deltas.rationale: When users request refunds to disallowed destinations, agents must both explain the limitation and get explicit confirmation of which allowed option to use before processing (observed: user requested refund to a different destination and the agent proceeded without explicit confirmation).

deltas.content: IF an exchange results in a price difference → THEN compute the difference, tell the user whether they owe money or will receive a refund (include the amount), ask which payment method to use for payment/refund, and explicitly ask the user to confirm the computed amount and payment method choice before executing any payment or exchange action.
deltas.rationale: Agents must verify and gain explicit confirmation of the computed price difference before charging or refunding; this prevents proceeding on incorrect amounts (observed: an incorrect difference was used without explicit confirmation).

deltas.content: IF executing any order-changing tool call → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id/get_user_details) → ask the user to specify/confirm the exact order_id to act on if multiple orders exist → fetch the confirmed order_details for that order_id → check product/variant availability and price → obtain/confirm payment method (and confirm any computed price difference with the user) → then call the appropriate action tool.
deltas.rationale: Ensure agents always confirm which order to change when users have multiple orders and follow the full verification sequence before making any modifications (observed: an action was applied to the wrong order).

deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents refusing requests to cancel a single item from a pending order and only supporting whole-order cancellation. Clarifies expected behavior for partial cancellations.

deltas.content: BEFORE calling any order-changing tool (exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, cancel_pending_order) → VERIFY that the order_id being used matches an order returned by get_order_details for the identified user and that every item_id listed in item_ids exists in that order's items. ALSO verify that each new_item_id is a valid variant item_id returned by get_product_details(product_id) and that its "available" field is true (or communicate if not available). If any mismatch or unavailable variant is detected, do not call the action tool; instead inform the user of the issue and request clarification or select a valid item/order.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory attempted a tool call using a product_id as an order_id and received an "Order not found" error. Explicit verification of order_id and item_ids before tool calls prevents such errors and enforces consistent mapping between selected variants and the target order.

deltas.rationale: Conversation evidence shows users asking to split payments and agents responding that this is not possible. The playbook lacks an explicit rule forbidding split payments and explaining how to handle such requests.

deltas.rationale: In some trajectories the agent proceeded using the single available payment method without prompting (conversation 7) and in others the user requested refund to the original method (conversation 96); the rule should allow these practical exceptions.

deltas.content: IF user requests cancellation → THEN ask for the reason for cancelling before calling cancel_pending_order. If the user supplies a reason in the same request (or earlier in the same conversation), use that reason verbatim when calling cancel_pending_order.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show cases where a user later provided a preferred cancel reason (conversation 76); the playbook should require agents to ask for a reason and to use the user's provided text as the cancel reason when available.

  [info-002] IF user refuses to provide zip code → THEN ask for the user's email address instead
  [info-003] IF provided first name, last name, and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip)
  [info-004] IF provided email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email)
  [info-005] IF find_user_id returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and payment methods
  [info-006] IF acting on a specific order or item → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each relevant order_id before taking action
  [info-007] IF user asks about product options or replacement variants → THEN call list_all_product_types() (if needed) and call get_product_details(product_id) to get variant availability, options, and prices
  [info-008] IF user requests an exchange for an item already in an order → THEN look up the product_id from the order details and call get_product_details(product_id) to identify suitable replacement item_ids

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF order status is "delivered" AND user requests an exchange of delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-002] IF order status is "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF order status is "pending" AND user requests to change item variants → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-004] IF order status is "pending" AND user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
  [acti-005] IF a replacement variant must be chosen → THEN verify variant availability and price via get_product_details(product_id) and confirm selection with the user before calling any exchange/modify tool
  [acti-006] IF an exchange/return involves a price difference → THEN include the payment_method_id in the exchange/return call and use the specified payment method to process the payment or refund
  [acti-007] IF executing any order-changing tool call → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id/get_user_details) → fetch relevant order_details → check product/variant availability and price → obtain/confirm payment method → then call the appropriate action tool

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF a replacement variant is found → THEN present the variant's size/compatibility/backlight/etc. and price and ask the user "Do you want this one?" before proceeding
  [conf-002] IF an exchange results in a price difference → THEN compute the difference, tell the user whether they owe money or will receive a refund (include the amount), and ask which payment method to use for payment/refund
  [conf-003] IF initiating a return or exchange → THEN tell the user they will receive an email with return instructions
  [conf-004] IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) has been successfully submitted → THEN inform the user it has been processed (e.g., "it's been processed" / "return requested" / "cancelled") and ask if they need anything else
  [conf-005] IF the user's requested refund destination is not allowed (e.g., different original payment methods across orders) → THEN explain the limitation and state the allowed options (refund to the original payment method or to a gift card)
  [conf-006] IF user requests cancellation → THEN ask for the reason for cancelling before calling cancel_pending_order

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF user identification (first name, last name, zip OR email) is not provided → THEN do not fetch or act on account/orders
  [prec-002] IF refund is requested → THEN refunds are only allowed to the original payment method used for that order or to a gift card; refunds cannot be routed to another order's different payment method
  [prec-003] IF order status is not "pending" → THEN do not call cancel_pending_order (only pending orders are cancellable)
  [prec-004] IF order status is "delivered" → THEN use return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items (do not use modify_pending_order_items for delivered orders)
  [prec-005] IF a desired replacement variant's "available" field is false → THEN inform the user that the variant is out of stock and do not select it for exchange/modify

deltas.content: IF refund is requested → THEN refunds for returns are only allowed to the original payment method used for that order or to a gift card; refunds resulting from exchanges (when the replacement is cheaper and an exchange_price_difference indicates a refund) may be issued to a different payment method on file at the customer's request. Full returns must follow the original-payment-or-gift-card restriction.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents enforcing original-payment-or-gift-card for full returns (e.g., gift-card paid orders refunded only to gift card) but allowing refunds arising from exchanges (price difference) to be issued to a different saved payment method when the user requested it.

deltas.content: IF user requests updating the shipping address for one or more pending orders or to change their default address → THEN verify user identity (per info-001/002/003/004), call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each relevant pending order_id, and if the user requests, call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account default address.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories include updates to pending order addresses and updating the user's default address via modify_pending_order_address and modify_user_address (conversation task_id 87), but the playbook lacks an explicit rule for address modifications.

deltas.content: IF provided email (even if name/zip also provided) → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email). Email lookup should be preferred when available because it is a unique identifier.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents using email-based lookup when email was provided; the playbook should state this preference.

deltas.content: IF user requests cancellation → THEN ask for the reason for cancelling before calling cancel_pending_order. If the user has already stated a reason earlier (e.g., 'no longer needed') treat that as the cancel reason. If the user confirms they want to proceed but does not provide a reason, it is acceptable to proceed using a default reason such as 'no longer needed' while offering the user a chance to provide a different reason.
deltas.rationale: In practice agents proceeded with cancellations after the user confirmed without explicitly asking for a reason; the rule should allow deriving or defaulting the reason while still soliciting one when appropriate.

deltas.content: IF a user requests escalation/transfer to a human agent (explicitly asks to be transferred or requests help the assistant cannot provide, e.g., to change refund destination outside allowed options) → THEN offer and initiate a transfer/escalation to a human agent, include a brief summary of the issue and relevant order details to the human agent, and confirm to the user that the transfer is being made.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory included a user request to be transferred to a human when the refund destination could not be changed; the playbook should include an explicit escalation/transfer step.

deltas.content: IF refund is requested → THEN refunds should by default be returned to the original payment method used for that order or to a gift card. If the user explicitly requests a different payment method that is associated with the same user account, the refund may be routed to that on-account payment method only after the agent confirms the user's explicit request and verifies the payment method is valid. Refunds cannot be routed to payment methods not associated with the user's account.
deltas.rationale: In multiple trajectories agents honored user requests to use a different payment method on the same account for refunds/exchanges; the original rule forbade routing refunds to a different payment method without allowing an on-account exception.

deltas.content: IF the user's requested refund destination is not allowed (e.g., a payment method not on the user's account or otherwise restricted) → THEN explain the limitation and state the allowed options (refund to the original payment method, to a gift card, or to an alternative on-account payment method only if the user explicitly requests and confirms it). If the user requests an alternative on-account payment method, confirm the choice before processing.
deltas.rationale: Agents sometimes accepted user requests to refund to a different on-account payment method without first explaining/confirming the limitation; update requires explicit confirmation when using an alternate on-account payment method and preserves explanation when a request is not allowed.

  [acti-002] IF order status is "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF order status is "pending" AND user requests to change item variants → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-004] IF order status is "pending" AND user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
  [acti-005] IF a replacement variant must be chosen → THEN verify variant availability and price via get_product_details(product_id) and confirm selection with the user before calling any exchange/modify tool
  [acti-006] IF an exchange/return involves a price difference → THEN include the payment_method_id in the exchange/return call and use the specified payment method to process the payment or refund
  [acti-007] IF executing any order-changing tool call → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id/get_user_details) → fetch relevant order_details → check product/variant availability and price → obtain/confirm payment method → then call the appropriate action tool

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF a replacement variant is found → THEN present the variant's size/compatibility/backlight/etc. and price and ask the user "Do you want this one?" before proceeding
  [conf-002] IF an exchange results in a price difference → THEN compute the difference, tell the user whether they owe money or will receive a refund (include the amount), and ask which payment method to use for payment/refund
  [conf-003] IF initiating a return or exchange → THEN tell the user they will receive an email with return instructions
  [conf-004] IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) has been successfully submitted → THEN inform the user it has been processed (e.g., "it's been processed" / "return requested" / "cancelled") and ask if they need anything else
  [conf-005] IF the user's requested refund destination is not allowed (e.g., different original payment methods across orders) → THEN explain the limitation and state the allowed options (refund to the original payment method or to a gift card)
  [conf-006] IF user requests cancellation → THEN ask for the reason for cancelling before calling cancel_pending_order

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF user identification (first name, last name, zip OR email) is not provided → THEN do not fetch or act on account/orders
  [prec-002] IF refund is requested → THEN refunds are only allowed to the original payment method used for that order or to a gift card; refunds cannot be routed to another order's different payment method
  [prec-003] IF order status is not "pending" → THEN do not call cancel_pending_order (only pending orders are cancellable)
  [prec-004] IF order status is "delivered" → THEN use return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items (do not use modify_pending_order_items for delivered orders)
  [prec-005] IF a desired replacement variant's "available" field is false → THEN inform the user that the variant is out of stock and do not select it for exchange/modify

deltas.content: IF order status is "pending" AND user requests an address change → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) after identifying the user and fetching the relevant order_details (follow the acti-007 sequence before making the change).
deltas.rationale: Agents modified the address of a pending order using modify_pending_order_address, but the playbook lacked a rule covering address changes for pending orders.

deltas.content: IF the user requests a tracking number for an order (including cancelled orders) → THEN fetch the order_details(order_id) and provide any available tracking_id(s) and the order status when responding.
deltas.rationale: A user asked for the tracking number of a cancelled order and the agent provided it; the playbook did not specify how to handle user requests for tracking numbers.

deltas.content: IF the user requests escalation or explicitly asks to speak to a human agent → THEN acknowledge the request and transfer the conversation to a human agent (log/record the user's reason if provided).
deltas.rationale: A user demanded a real person and the agent transferred the conversation; playbook should include an escalation path when users ask for a human.

deltas.content: IF multiple refunds/cancellations/returns are being processed for a user in a single interaction → THEN present a clear itemized breakdown showing each order, the amount being refunded now (versus any amounts already refunded), and the total refund amount.
deltas.rationale: Agents computed and reported a total refund across multiple orders; playbook should require an itemized breakdown and total when multiple refunds/cancellations are handled.

bullets.content: IF user requests account-specific actions (exchange/return/modify/cancel) → THEN ask for first name, last name, and zip code before proceeding
IF user refuses to provide zip code → THEN ask for the user's email address instead

IF an email is provided (even if name/zip are also provided) THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email); otherwise, if first name, last name, and zip are provided, call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip).
IF find_user_id returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and payment methods
IF acting on a specific order or item → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each relevant order_id before taking action

IF user asks about product options, replacement variants, or how many product options/variants are available → THEN call list_all_product_types() (if needed) and call get_product_details(product_id); use get_product_details to obtain variant availability, options, and prices, and if the user asked for a count, report the number of variants whose 'available' field is true.
IF user requests an exchange for an item already in an order → THEN look up the product_id from the order details and call get_product_details(product_id) to identify suitable replacement item_ids
IF order status is "delivered" AND user requests an exchange of delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order status is "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order status is "pending" AND user requests to change item variants → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)

IF order status is "pending" AND user requests cancellation → THEN if the user requests cancellation of the entire order, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). Do not cancel only part of a pending order; if the user asks to cancel some items from a pending order, inform them partial cancellations are not allowed for pending orders and offer alternatives (e.g., change the delivery address for the pending order or return the unwanted items after delivery).
IF a replacement variant must be chosen → THEN verify variant availability and price via get_product_details(product_id) and confirm selection with the user before calling any exchange/modify tool
IF an exchange/return involves a price difference → THEN include the payment_method_id in the exchange/return call and use the specified payment method to process the payment or refund

IF executing any order-changing tool call → THEN perform the sequence: identify user (find_user_id/get_user_details) → ask the user to specify/confirm the exact order_id to act on if multiple orders exist → fetch the confirmed order_details for that order_id → check product/variant availability and price → obtain/confirm payment method (and confirm any computed price difference with the user) → then call the appropriate action tool.

IF a replacement variant is found → THEN present the variant's size/compatibility/backlight/etc. and price and ask the user 'Do you want this one?' before proceeding, UNLESS the user has explicitly requested that specific variant/item_id or has already explicitly confirmed that exact replacement — in that case, present the variant details and proceed without an additional yes/no prompt.

IF an exchange results in a price difference → THEN compute the difference, tell the user whether they owe money or will receive a refund (include the amount), and ask which payment method to use for payment/refund. EXCEPTION: if there is only one valid payment method on the user's account, or the refund must be returned to the original payment method (per precondition rules), proceed using that payment method and inform the user instead of asking.

IF initiating a return or exchange → THEN tell the user they will receive an email with return/exchange instructions; also confirm the refund/payment destination (original payment method or gift card) and, if the user asked about timing, provide the expected refund/payment timing.

IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) has been successfully submitted → THEN inform the user it has been processed (e.g., "it's been processed" / "return requested" / "cancelled") and ask if they need anything else

IF the user's requested refund destination is not allowed (e.g., a payment method not on the user's account or otherwise restricted) → THEN explain the limitation and state the allowed options (refund to the original payment method, to a gift card, or to an alternative on-account payment method only if the user explicitly requests and confirms it). If the user requests an alternative on-account payment method, confirm the choice before processing.

IF user requests cancellation → THEN ask for the reason for cancelling before calling cancel_pending_order. If the user does not provide a reason when asked, the agent MAY proceed using a clear default reason (for example: 'no longer needed') when submitting the cancel_pending_order call.
IF user identification (first name, last name, zip OR email) is not provided → THEN do not fetch or act on account/orders

IF refund is requested → THEN refunds should by default be returned to the original payment method used for that order or to a gift card. If the user explicitly requests a different payment method that is associated with the same user account, the refund may be routed to that on-account payment method only after the agent confirms the user's explicit request and verifies the payment method is valid. Refunds cannot be routed to payment methods not associated with the user's account.

IF user requests cancellation → THEN only pending orders can be cancelled, and cancel_pending_order cancels the entire order (cancellation of individual items within an order is not supported). If the user wants to remove or change a single item, offer alternatives: cancel the full order, use modify_pending_order_items to change item variants on pending orders, or initiate a return for delivered items.
IF order status is "delivered" → THEN use return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items (do not use modify_pending_order_items for delivered orders)
IF a desired replacement variant's "available" field is false → THEN inform the user that the variant is out of stock and do not select it for exchange/modify

IF the user requests updating the shipping address for one or more orders and/or changing their account/default address → THEN perform the following sequence: verify user identity (per info-001/002/003/004); identify the user (e.g., find_user_id_by_name_zip or find_user_id_by_email → get_user_details); fetch relevant order_details for each relevant order_id and confirm each order's status; for each order with status "pending" call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip); if the user requests updating their account/default address, call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip); for orders not in "pending" status, inform the user those orders' shipping addresses cannot be modified and offer alternatives (e.g., contact carrier, initiate return/exchange after delivery); confirm or obtain any needed payment method or additional confirmations before performing updates.
IF user requests an address change → THEN ask whether to apply the change to all pending orders and whether to update the user account address as well before making any modify calls.

IF order status is not 'pending' → THEN do not call modify_pending_order_address; inform the user that only pending orders' shipping addresses can be changed, that processed or delivered orders cannot have their shipping address modified, and provide available alternatives.

IF the user cannot complete payment through stored payment methods and requests to provide card details outside the chat (e.g., over the phone) OR does not want to save card information online → THEN offer to transfer the user to a human agent to complete the payment securely, and explain that this option is available if they prefer not to add payment details in-chat.

IF the user refuses to provide a new shipping address aloud but indicates a desired address is already saved in their account → THEN look up the user's saved addresses and offer the available saved addresses (or the specific saved address the user referenced) for selection/confirmation; if the user explicitly authorizes using one of the saved addresses, proceed to modify the order address using that saved address.

IF initiating a return or refund → THEN compute and tell the user the exact refund amount AND provide an estimated processing time for the refund to reach the chosen/original payment method (e.g., 'it can be done within X days'), and confirm the refund destination.

IF a user requests to use a gift card to pay for an exchange/return or to cover a price difference → THEN check the user's saved payment methods for a gift_card entry and verify the gift card's balance is sufficient to cover the required amount. If the balance is insufficient, inform the user of the shortfall and ask which alternate payment method they want to use (or whether to split payment). Confirm the chosen payment method before proceeding.

IF order status is 'pending' AND user requests to change the shipping/delivery address for a specific order → THEN verify user identity (e.g., first name + last name + zip OR email) → call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm order status and current address → IF order status is 'pending' AND the user confirms the new address → call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address before fulfillment → confirm the updated shipping address (and updated total, if applicable) with the user.

IF a user requests to update their account's default shipping address → THEN verify and identify the user (for example, by first name + last name + ZIP or by email, and/or by calling find_user_id/get_user_details), confirm the new address details with the user, call update_user_default_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) or modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) (or the equivalent tool) to set the new default address, and confirm to the user that the default account shipping address has been updated for future orders and advise any additional steps if needed.

IF a user has multiple orders and requests an account-specific action on an order or item → THEN ask the user to specify which order (order ID) they want to act on or explicitly confirm which of their orders is the target before fetching order details or making any changes.

IF a user requests an exchange to a different product category/type (e.g., luggage -> coat) → THEN inform the user that exchanges are limited to variants of the same product/product type (i.e., same product_id variants) and offer to process a return or cancel the order and place a new order for the different product type instead.

IF order status is 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt to reinstate the order via automated tools; inform the user that cancelled orders cannot be reopened automatically and offer to place a new order or escalate to a human agent for special handling.

BEFORE calling any order-changing tool (exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, cancel_pending_order) → VERIFY that the order_id being used matches an order returned by get_order_details for the identified user and that every item_id listed in item_ids exists in that order's items. ALSO verify that each new_item_id is a valid variant item_id returned by get_product_details(product_id) and that its "available" field is true (or communicate if not available). If any mismatch or unavailable variant is detected, do not call the action tool; instead inform the user of the issue and request clarification or select a valid item/order.

IF user requests splitting payment for a single order across multiple payment methods → THEN split payments are not supported. Inform the user that we cannot split a single order payment across multiple methods and offer alternatives (use another single payment method on file, cancel or modify the order to reduce the total, or place a new order for remaining items).
IF user requests tracking number(s) for an order → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) and return the tracking_id(s) found in the order's fulfillments to the user.

IF a gift card payment_method_id is to be used to cover a price difference for an exchange/modify → THEN verify the gift card balance is sufficient before calling the exchange/modify tool; if the balance is insufficient, inform the user and request an alternate payment method or present options (partial gift-card use plus another payment method).
IF an exchange/modify is charged to a gift card → THEN compute the remaining gift card balance after the charge and tell the user the remaining balance.

IF a user requests escalation/transfer to a human agent (explicitly asks to be transferred or requests help the assistant cannot provide, e.g., to change refund destination outside allowed options) → THEN offer and initiate a transfer/escalation to a human agent, include a brief summary of the issue and relevant order details to the human agent, and confirm to the user that the transfer is being made.

IF the user requests a tracking number for an order (including cancelled orders) → THEN fetch the order_details(order_id) and provide any available tracking_id(s) and the order status when responding.
IF the user requests escalation or explicitly asks to speak to a human agent → THEN acknowledge the request and transfer the conversation to a human agent (log/record the user's reason if provided).

IF multiple refunds/cancellations/returns are being processed for a user in a single interaction → THEN present a clear itemized breakdown showing each order, the amount being refunded now (versus any amounts already refunded), and the total refund amount.
